name: nasa-cmapss

data_origin.blend: C-MAPSS turbofan-engine simulator with Wiener-process-like degradation model

facts: FD001-FD004 simulating turbofan run-to-failure with operating-conditions × fault-modes factorial (1×1, 6×1, 1×2, 6×2).
hypotheses: The (op-condition × fault-mode) factorization is not orthogonal in the simulator's output: interactions dominate one axis.
Method ordering on CMAPSS does not predict ordering on N-CMAPSS (its more realistic NASA successor), only ordering on yet-more-CMAPSS.
intuitions: Cross-subset transfer surveys from 2023-25 and CMAPSS-vs-N-CMAPSS empirical comparison papers likely exist.
Degradation-model-ablation work that varies the simulator's Wiener-process assumption probably exists.
unknowns: The simulator's degradation model (Wiener-process-like with thresholds) is a representational commitment baked into every datum; whether real engine degradation actually follows this form cannot be determined from CMAPSS.
The 'fault mode' labels presuppose the same underlying physics across instances — also untestable from inside.
epistemic_state.boundary: Simulated data — every claim using CMAPSS is conditional on the simulator's structural choices; cannot stand as evidence for generalization to real data.

representational_commitments: 21 sensors selected (others in the simulator omitted)
4 subset definitions are the curator's factorial design
